prompt.content: Identify and list each set of end-of-line rhyming words, the rhyme scheme, alliteration, allusion, imagery, repetition, hyperboles, and personification in the poem "The Butterfly". If there are no relevant examples of the requested literary device in the poem, respond “No examples.”

rubrics.name: Lists "No example" for the rhyme scheme (ver_775d00a6a9374085921b980e930d6b8b)

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
Rhyming word pairs:  (high, good-bye)

Rhyme Scheme:  No examples.

Alliteration examples: 
Perhaps if the sun’s tears would sing against a white stone . . .

Allusion examples:
Penned up inside this ghetto
In the ghetto

Imagery examples:
So richly, brightly, dazzingly, yellow.
Perhaps if the sun's tears would sing against a white stone
The dandelions call to me
And the white chestnut branches in the court.

Repetition examples:
The last, the very last,
Such, such a  yellow
For seven weeks I’ve lived in here, /But I have found what I love here

Hyperbole examples:
So richly, brightly, dazzingly yellow. Perhaps if the sun’s tears would sing against a white stone. 

Personification examples:
Perhaps if the sun’s tears would sing"
It went away I’m sure because it wished to kiss the world good-bye.
The dandelions call to me
